privacy.note: Google Analytics and New Relic monitoring present. Standard nonprofit analytics. No ad-network data sharing or cross-domain profiling. No ad-supported model.

tos.note: Not directly evaluated. No modifier applied.

accessibility.note: No published accessibility statement found. Standard WordPress theme. Insufficient information to score positively or negatively.

mission.note: 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded 1922. Mission: unite writers and allies to celebrate creative expression and defend liberties. Explicit free expression and human rights mandate. PEN International member.

editorial_code.note: Original research with named methodology (20 interviews, digital research, public records). Partner attribution. Clear sourcing standards for HR organization.

ownership.note: Nonprofit 501(c)(3). Board of 41 independent trustees. Charity Navigator 3/4 stars (86%). Governance policies: conflict of interest, whistleblower, document retention. No asset diversion.

access_model.note: No paywall. No registration required. Reports and research published openly. Full open access.

ad_tracking.note: No advertising. No ad networks. Standard analytics only (Google Analytics, New Relic). Nonprofit model eliminates ad-driven data incentives.

scores.note: Centers human dignity, ethnic minority rights, rule of law. Documents violations against Mongolian people. Calls for rights-based response. S: open access, nonprofit model. Mission modifier +0.15.
Frames Mongolians as equal rights-holders. Amplifies affected community voices (exile journalist Soyonbo Borjgin). Solidarity framing.
Core content: documents ethnic discrimination against Mongolians by Chinese state. Systematic targeting based on ethnicity and language. Explicit advocacy against discrimination.
Documents mass detentions: 8,000-10,000 Mongolians in police custody following 2020 protests. Threats against educators and activists.
No direct content on slavery or servitude.
Documents forced re-education of Mongolian activists and educators. Forced public confessions on digital platforms. Degrading treatment.
No direct content on legal personhood.
Documents unequal treatment: Mongolians targeted with language restrictions not imposed on Han Chinese. Implicit advocacy for equal protection.
No direct content on judicial remedies. Implicitly relevant but too indirect.
Documents mass arbitrary detention: 8,000-10,000 in custody for peaceful protests against education policy changes.
No direct content on fair hearings.
No direct content on presumption of innocence.

Documents digital surveillance: WeChat monitoring (11/18 interviewees report restrictions), keyword censorship, transnational reach (police contact diaspora via WeChat in Japan, Australia, Canada, Europe). Advocates against privacy violations. S: pen.org uses only standard analytics, no ad networks. Ad/tracking modifier +0.05.
Documents restrictions on Mongolians' communication. Reports exile journalist displacement. Diaspora subject to transnational surveillance. Access modifier +0.15.
References Mongolians in exile (Soyonbo Borjgin in New York; communities in Japan, Australia, Canada, Europe). Implies asylum/refuge context. Indirect.
No direct content on nationality rights.
No content on marriage or family.
No direct content on property rights.
Documents suppression of Mongolian intellectual expression as thought/conscience violation. Language erasure linked to freedom of thought. Government labels language defense 'ideological warfare.'

Central topic. 89% of Mongolian cultural websites censored/shut down. Bainu app (400K users) restricted. Mongolian-script messaging apps restricted. Chat forums shut down. Music removed from apps. 'One Province One Newspaper One Client' crowds out independent platforms. PEN America's core mission is free expression. Context: ed. code +0.10, ownership +0.15, access +0.15 = +0.30 (at cap). Clamped to 0.90.
Documents suppression of Mongolian assembly: 300,000 students boycotted classes; mass detentions followed. Online meeting spaces shut down. Community forums closed.
No direct content on political participation.
Documents destruction of Mongolian cultural infrastructure online. 'Northern Frontier culture' rebranding eliminates Mongolian identity from institutions.
No direct content on work or equal pay.
No content on rest and leisure.
No content on standard of living.

Documents destruction of Mongolian-language education: 2020 policy replaced Mongolian with Mandarin across all subjects. 300,000 students boycotted. Educators terminated. Report title 'Save Our Mother Tongue' centers education rights.

Directly addresses cultural participation: 89% of cultural websites shut down; Mongolian music removed from streaming; playlists rebranded with communist themes; institutions renamed to erase identity. S: pen.org makes research freely available.

Calls for international response from tech companies, governments, international institutions. Frames issue as requiring just international order. PEN International membership. S: nonprofit models international solidarity. Mission modifier +0.15.

Frames tech companies as having duties: 'adopt cultural rights framework,' 'respect human rights responsibilities,' 'partner with independent organizations.' Invokes corporate duties to community. S: PEN operates as accountable nonprofit (Charity Navigator 86%, governance transparency).

Directly addresses state destruction of minority rights. Chinese government campaign constitutes exactly the activity Art. 30 prohibits: state action aimed at destruction of rights. PEN explicitly advocates against this.
